message:
Enhance benchmark governance with comparison context and triage followup commands

perf_comparison.rs:
- Add ComparisonContext struct carrying benchmark_id, profile_id, and
  baseline_ref for structured logging of comparison decisions, enabling
  post-mortem correlation of regression alerts to specific benchmarks.
- Add compare_with_context() method to RegressionComparator that accepts
  an optional ComparisonContext, extending the existing compare() method
  (which now delegates to it with None context) without breaking callers.
- Structured log events now include benchmark_id, profile_id, and
  baseline_ref fields when context is provided.
- Add tests: context-aware comparison produces identical verdicts to the
  context-free path, and ComparisonContext JSON round-trips correctly.

perf_triage.rs:
- Add followup_command field to TriageDecision providing a deterministic
  CLI repro command for each triage action, so operators can immediately
  rerun without reading the runbook.
- Add followup_for() function mapping each TriageAction to a concrete
  shell command invoking benchmark_record.sh with the failing operation
  ID and appropriate flags (--runs, --compare-baseline, --out-json).
- Wire followup_command into all TriageDecision construction sites
  (classify_triage and all early-exit paths) and into emit_triage_log.
- Add 5 tests: non-empty followup for actionable decisions, empty
  followup for noise, benchmark_record reference for CollectMoreSamples,
  --runs flag presence, and JSON serialization includes the field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
